chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يَتْبَعُ جَنَازَةً ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – The one who follows a coffin, then returns

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/17)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/17)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَمِيرَانِ وَ لَيْسَا بِأَمِيرَيْنِ لَيْسَ لِمَنْ تَبِعَ جَنَازَةً أَنْ يَرْجِعَ حَتَّى يُدْفَنَ أَوْ يُؤْذَنَ لَهُ وَ رَجُلٌ يَحُجُّ مَعَ امْرَأَةٍ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ أَنْ يَنْفِرَ حَتَّى تَقْضِيَ نُسُكَهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) raising it from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Two Emirs (ones with command) are not with two commands – it is not for the one who follows a coffin that he returns until it is permitted for him, and a man who performs Hajj with a woman, so it is not for him that he flees (abandons her) until she has fulfilled her rituals’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah in a marfu‘ manner who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah has said, ‘Two kinds of commanders have no command. One is he who joins a funeral procession. He has no command to return home before the burial is complete, or he is given permission to return. The other is a man who performs Hajj along with a woman. He has no command to leave her alone before she completes all activities of her Hajj.’”
